title: sphaeralcea orbiculata subsp. clementina
intro: Sphaeralcea orbiculata subsp. clementina, commonly known as the globemallow, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a subspecies of the sphaeralcea orbiculata, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The globemallow is used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers are often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other ailments.
